title: Daily Briefing — May 15
headline: Tech's leading, but sector rotation screams lag — don't chase, follow the flows.
body: Right, so here's the big picture: SPY pushing up to $748.17, well above the 20 MA at $722.35. Tech (XLK, NVDA, ARM) remains king, with XLK at $179.50 — that's a solid premium to the mean reversion zone, and the flows are still bullish there. But the majority of sectors are lagging or carrying elevated risk, and VIX is creeping above its 20-day, hinting at expanding volatility. Fear & Greed sits at 65 — not extreme, but inching closer to danger.

Key sector insight: XLK continues to lead (RS=117.0), driven by monster moves from NVDA ($235.74, +4.4%, SMA20 at $209.12) and ARM ($228.50, +3.3%, SMA20 at $206.86) — these setups are still active, so strength there is real. XLF (Financials) is improving but still lagging long-term (SMA200 at $52.27, current $51.29), while everything else — Energy, Industrials, Healthcare, Staples — is either lagging or risk elevated. Rotation hasn't followed tech's momentum; breadth is a concern.

Contrarian/risk flag: Bonds (HYG $79.85, LQD $108.55, TLT $84.92) are flirting with bearish signals — TLT triggered below its 20 MA, and HYG is hugging the breakdown zone at SMA20 ($79.99). If bonds freak out, everything breaks — watch these closely. Most sectors are high risk, and retail is still piling in on tech. If consensus is all-in tech, be suspicious — cycles within cycles, mean reversion is overdue.
callout_body:
SPY: $748.17 (20 MA: $722.35, 50 MA: $688.48)
XLK: $179.50 (20 MA: $164.45)
NVDA: $235.74 (Watch the 20 MA: $209.12)
XLF: $51.29 (SMA200: $52.27 — if it breaks, trouble)
HYG: $79.85 (20 MA: $79.99 — bonds at risk)
VIX: 19.02 (SMA20: 18.08 — volatility expanding)
action_items: Maintain exposure in XLK leaders (NVDA, ARM, DELL) so long as daily closes hold above the 20 MA.
Monitor bonds (HYG, TLT, LQD) — any confirmed breakdown triggers Rule R001: reduce equity exposure immediately.
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Abundance mindset — next trade.
